t_ch: 唔…「欢迎来到昏光庭院。请问有什么可以帮到您？」
t_en: Hmm... "Welcome to the Twilight Courtyard. How can I help you?"

t_ch: 感觉阳雷骑士大人帮不到我。
t_en: I don't think the Daythunder Knight can help me.

t_ch: 几天不见，变这么小了？
t_en: It's only been a few days since we last saw each other. How'd you get so tiny?

t_ch: 是你啊，曾和雅辛忒丝一同重登天空的人子，幸能再会。
t_en: Ah, it's you. The child of humanity who ascended to the skies alongside Hyacinthia. It is a pleasure to meet you again.

t_ch: 吾如今返璞归真，不再是手持枪矛的女武神，亦非明镜高悬的融合神，仅是一位平凡的女童。
t_en: I have now returned to my original, pure state. I am no longer a warrior goddess wielding a spear, nor the all-seeing Theos Synthetos. I am but an ordinary young girl.

t_ch: 哈哈…话虽如此，塞涅俄丝大人说话的口吻，一时半会儿还是改不过来呢……
t_en: Haha... Even so, Lady Seliose's way of speaking proved quite persistent...

t_ch: …其实，我倒觉得塞涅俄丝如今这般…不失为一种反差的美感。
t_en: ...Actually, I think Seliose's current state has... a certain beauty in its contrast.

t_ch: 同感。正如那践行时分，于明媚午后降落的一场太阳雨……
t_en: I feel the same. Just like during that Action Hour, when a sunshower fell on that bright afternoon...

t_ch: 嘟，嘟嘟！
t_en: Doot, doot-doot!

t_ch: 呀！嘘，小伊卡，不可以乱说话……
t_en: Ah! Shh, Little Ica, you shouldn't say things like that...

t_ch: 不必遮掩，吾听得懂翼兽的言语。「外表看似孩童，年纪却远超常人……」
t_en: No need to hide it, I can understand the winged beast's words. "Despite her appearance as a child, her age far exceeds that of ordinary people..."

t_ch: 天马没说错。尽管岁数见长，吾的心智却未必有雅辛忒丝那般成熟。
t_en: The pegasus is right. Despite my age, my mind may not be as mature as Hyacinthia's.

t_ch: 此番加入昏光庭院，正是想成为一名医师，研习与人子心灵相通的方法。
t_en: I joined the Twilight Courtyard precisely because I wanted to become a physician and study ways to connect with the hearts of the children of humanity.

t_ch: 塞涅俄丝大人有这样的想法与愿景…真好。不过，成为一名医师可是很难的哦，要学习满满当当整本手册的知识，学会应对各种情景。
t_en: It's wonderful that Lady Seliose has such thoughts and aspirations. However, it is no easy feat to become a doctor. You'll need to master entire manuals' worth of knowledge and learn to handle all sorts of situations.

t_ch: 比如…要是遇到病人在庭院胡闹，该怎么处理呢？
t_en: For example... what should we do if we encounter patients causing trouble in the courtyard?

t_ch: 胡闹？熔…咳，融入他们的内心，知晓他们的诉求，对确有困难者耐心疏导，对无理取闹者礼貌劝离。
t_en: Causing trouble? Melt... *ahem* Meld with their hearts, understand their needs, patiently guide those with genuine difficulties, and politely turn away those who make unreasonable demands.

t_ch: 你刚刚先说了熔对吧？
t_en: You were gonna say "melt," didn't you?

t_ch: 不懂医术，略懂拳脚？
t_en: You're not much of a doctor, but know a thing or two about throwing punches?

t_ch: …休要胡说，吾明明回答得一字不差。
t_en: ...Don't be ridiculous! I did not misspeak.

t_ch: 啊哈哈…塞涅俄丝大人很有天分的，假以时日，一定会成为一名优秀的医师！
t_en: Ahaha... Lady Seliose is very talented. With time, she'll definitely become an excellent physician!

t_ch: 嘟嘟！
t_en: Doot-doot!

t_ch: 雅辛忒丝说，吾有一颗赤子之心。希望…吾不会让她失望。
t_en: Hyacinthia says I have a pure and innocent heart. I hope... I won't let her down.

t_ch: 塞涅俄丝大人的加入，为我揭示了一种全新的可能：也许，我可以帮助人们成为自己的医生、疗愈自身……
t_en: Lady Seliose's arrival has revealed a whole new possibility to me: Perhaps, I can help people become their own doctors, to heal themselves...

t_ch: 不过要是那样，昏光庭院发挥的场所可就更少啦。
t_en: But if that happens, there would be a lesser need for the Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 昏光庭院该何去何从…
t_en: What will become of the Twilight Courtyard...

t_ch: 听说，有句来自天外的箴言是这样的：「但愿世间人无病，宁可架上药生尘」。也许，这就是所有医者应当具备的心境。
t_en: They say there's a proverb from beyond the sky that goes: "May all people be free from illness, even if it means medicines gather dust on the shelves." Perhaps this is the mindset that all Healers should possess.

t_ch: 我想成为最幸福的失业者。就让昏光庭院归于平凡，成为大家的下午茶沙龙吧！
t_en: I want to be the happiest unemployed person ever. Let the Twilight Courtyard return to normal and become everyone's afternoon tea salon!

t_ch: 会不会太勉强塞涅俄丝了…
t_en: Will we be pushing Seliose too hard...?

t_ch: 开始确实有些艰难。塞涅俄丝大人缺少和患者相处的经验，难以理解常人的情绪，也没法用合适的方式将情绪表达出来。
t_en: It will be quite difficult at first. Lady Seliose lacks experience in dealing with patients, finding it hard to understand ordinary people's emotions, and is unable to express her own feelings appropriately.

t_ch: 但她拥有成为一名医者最为重要的特质——那就是一颗赤子之心。
t_en: But she possesses the most essential quality of a healer: a pure and innocent heart.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 希望铭记的东西…呀，看灰宝期盼的表情，是想要翁法罗斯纪念品，对吧？
t_en: Something to remember...? Oh, judging by Grayie's eager expression, you want an Amphoreus souvenir, right?

t_ch: 正好，瞧，这是昏光庭院的新产品——小伊卡限量周边！本来已经被抢完了，幸好我自己还留着一个，灰宝就把它捎回家吧！
t_en: Perfect timing! Look, this is Twilight Courtyard's new product, Limited Edition Little Ica merch! They were all sold out, but luckily I kept one for myself. Here, Grayie, take it home with you!

t_ch: 最精妙的设计是，只要轻轻一捏，它就能用小伊卡的声音唱歌哦！你听——嘟嘟嘟嘟，嘟~嘟~嘟嘟——
t_en: The most ingenious part of the design is that with just a gentle squeeze, it can sing in Little Ica's voice! Listen—Doot-doot-doot-doot, doot~doot~doot-doot—

t_ch: 嘻，是不是特别地可爱？
t_en: Hehe, isn't it super adorable?

t_ch: 我还会来庭院做客的。
t_en: I'll come visit the courtyard again.

t_ch: 好呀！我会为灰宝准备好茶点的，要常来哦！
t_en: Great! I'll prepare some snacks and tea for Grayie, so make sure to visit often!

t_ch: 思维枝椁、大地晶矿、悬锋钢，再缀以少许黄金……
t_en: Bough of Thought, Earth Crystal Ore, Kremnoan steel, with a touch of gold...

t_ch: 用料殊异如此之大，容吾猜测…你是炼成捍卫树庭的战略武器，亦或是钻研来自繁星的前沿科技…？
t_en: With such peculiar materials in such quantities, let me guess... Are you crafting a strategic weapon to protect the Grove, or perhaps researching cutting-edge technology from the stars...?

t_ch: 都不是。没想到大名鼎鼎的瑟希斯，在如此显而易见的成果面前栽了跟头。
t_en: Neither. Who would've thought the great Cerces would stumble over such obvious results.

t_ch: 吾师卡吕普索啊，不妨修剪一番你那过度发散的思维，裁掉冗余之物，再仔细看看。
t_en: Master Calypso, why not prune those overly divergent thoughts of yours, cut away the redundancies, and take another careful look?

t_ch: 好哇，小夏可真是有派头。不如让姐姐来猜猜？
t_en: Well, well, look who's putting on airs, Naxy! Shall I take a guess?

t_ch: 树枝、亮晶晶的石头、铁丝，还有金色的鳞粉……
t_en: A twig, shiny stone, wire, and golden scale...

t_ch: 咦，小夏，我记得你小时候送我的…发卡，就是用这些材料做的吧？
t_en: Hey Naxy, wasn't... the hairpin you gave me as a kid made from these materials?

t_ch: …答对了，不过你本来知道答案。
t_en: ...Correct, but you already knew the answer.

t_ch: 果真是发卡？人子，你要用炼金术做发卡？！
t_en: A hairpin? For real? Child of humanity, you want to use alchemy to make a hairpin!?

t_ch: 是又如何？万人景仰的理性之泰坦，莫不是个古板迂腐之徒吧？
t_en: So what if I am? Don't tell me the revered Reason Titan is conservative and old-fashioned?

t_ch: …不，人子，譬喻而言，倘若悬锋人见到自己千锤百锻的锋刃，被庖厨用以切斫肉糜，该作何感想？
t_en: ...No, child of humanity. Let me put it this way. How would a Kremnoan feel if they saw their meticulously forged blade being used by a cook to mince meat?

t_ch: 你还是见识短浅了。猜猜看，如今悬锋之王迈德漠斯下厨时，用的是什么刀具？
t_en: You're still quite narrow-minded. Care to guess what kind of knife the King of Kremnos, Mydeimos, uses when he cooks these days?

t_ch: …吾无话可说。
t_en: ...I'm speechless.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈…至是，工程已毕——喏，收好了。
t_en: Hahaha... Well, it's done. Here, take good care of it.

t_ch: 材料还蛮多的，也送我一枚吧。
t_en: You've got plenty of materials there, toss one my way too.

t_ch: 呵呵，不过…送你自是简单，但你若是学会自己炼成，岂不是更好？
t_en: Hehe, I could simply give it to you, but... wouldn't it be better if you learned to craft it yourself?

t_ch: 以为搓个核弹，结果捏个发夹。
t_en: Thought you were making a nuclear bomb, but turns out it was just a hairpin.

t_ch: 呵呵，若是搓出了核弹，学者们便又要来咄咄逼人了，一枚发卡岂不是正好？
t_en: Hehe, if I end up creating a nuclear bomb, those scholars are going to get on my case again. A hairpin would be just perfect, wouldn't it?

t_ch: 任何微渺的炼金造术，都须持有足够严谨的学术态度。喏，姐姐，这是送给你的……
t_en: Even the smallest act of alchemy requires a rigorous academic approach. Here, sister, this is for you...

t_ch: 啊…真好看啊，尤其是发夹中间的那颗水晶，简直像你的眼睛…谢谢你，小夏！
t_en: Oh... It's so beautiful, especially that crystal in the middle of the hairpin. It's just like your eyes... Thank you, Naxy!

t_ch: 举手之劳，你喜欢就好，不知道它是否和我记忆中别无二致呢。
t_en: It's a piece of cake, I'm glad you like it. I wonder if it's exactly as I remember it.

t_ch: 就算有差别也无妨，我们都没有活在过去嘛。
t_en: It's fine even if there are differences. We don't live in the past.

t_ch: …对了，你还没和我说你这段时间的经历呢，怎么人家都喊你「大表演家」「穿着华服的大地兽」？我好好奇呀！
t_en: ...Oh right, you still haven't told me about what you've been up to lately! How come everyone's calling you "the Great Performer" and "a dromas draped in finery"? I'm so curious!

t_ch: …唉，姐姐，我要去备课了，这个问题就留给你自己吧，记住不可相信多嘴多舌之人。
t_en: ...Ah, sister, I need to go prepare for my class. I'll leave this matter to you. Just remember not to trust those who gossip too much.

t_ch: 哎呀，人子唷，身为神悟树庭教授，可不能将求知若渴者拒之门外啊…小姑娘，过来吧，汝大可相信多嘴多舌之神，吾来答疑解惑。
t_en: Oh my, child of humanity! As a professor of the Grove of Epiphany, I simply cannot turn away those who thirst for knowledge... Come closer, young lady. You can trust this chatty God. I shall answer all your questions and clear your doubts.

t_ch: 卡吕普索女士，你肯定知道不少小夏长大后的故事吧？请务必都讲给我听！
t_en: Ms. Calypso, you must know a lot of stories about Naxy growing up! Please, you absolutely have to tell me all about them!

t_ch: 呵呵，吾翻动过他的脑子…啊，领教过他的智慧，自当知无不言。首先要说的，便是他穿着大地兽睡衣，在敬拜学派的礼会上当众致辞一事……
t_en: Hehe, I've flipped through his mind... Ah, I've experienced his wisdom firsthand, so I shall tell you everything. The first thing worth mentioning is how he gave a public speech at a Venerationism ceremony wearing dromas pajamas...

t_ch: 呵…希望卡吕普索那家伙管好话匣子，不要跟我姐姐说一些无趣的往事。
t_en: Heh... I hope that Calypso keeps her mouth shut and doesn't bore my sister with stories from the past.

t_ch: 你姐姐叫你小夏也不生气？
t_en: You don't mind your sister calling you Naxy?

t_ch: 我倒是希望她能理解我已经不再是个乳臭未干的小孩了…不过，那毕竟是家人，她愿意怎么叫就怎么叫吧，难道你能要求你的家人？
t_en: I wish she could understand that I'm not some kid who's still wet-behind-the-ears... But then again, she's family. Let her call me whatever she wants. Can you really make demands of your own family?

t_ch: 我的家人都叫我宝宝。
t_en: My family calls me baby.

t_ch: …那我还能说什么呢？换位思考一下，我叫你宝宝你会开心吗？
t_en: ...Well, what would you have me say? Put yourself in my shoes, then. Would you be happy if I called you "baby"?

t_ch: 开心，请你现在就这样叫。
t_en: Yes. Please call me that from now on.

t_ch: 看来这是个体差异，人与人之间的鸿沟。
t_en: Looks like it's just individual differences — a gulf between people.

t_ch: 怎么说呢，感觉有些微妙…
t_en: How should I put this... It feels a little awkward...

t_ch: 很高兴我们达成了共识。
t_en: I'm glad we've reached an agreement.

t_ch: 现在，请你就适当代入下我，我姐姐可以叫我宝宝，别人不可以。
t_en: Now, try to put yourself in my shoes. My sister can call me "baby," but no one else can.

t_ch: 家人之间也必须尊重彼此感受。
t_en: Family members must also respect each other's feelings.

t_ch: 确实，你说的没错。我承认了，我喜欢姐姐叫我「小夏」，这是我的感受。
t_en: Indeed, you're right. I'll admit, I like it when my sister calls me "Naxy." That's just how I feel.

t_ch: 至于「那刻夏」，它看上去仅仅是一个称呼，实际上它是一次「认知」调整。
t_en: As for "Anaxa," while it appears to be just a name, it is in fact an adjustment of "Cognition."

t_ch: 就像我们会在别人的名字后面加上「教授」、「老师」…等一系列称呼，对话也能建立在「与学者对话」的认知中，它潜移默化但行之有效。
t_en: Just as we add titles like "Professor" or "Teacher" before someone's name, conversations can be built on the premise of "talking to a scholar." Subtle, yet effective.

t_ch: 所以，能够以「阿那克萨戈拉斯」称呼我的话，如此的对话多半不是无效的——毕竟对方是一个能够照顾你的感受，并且为之做出让步的人。
t_en: So, if someone addresses me as "Anaxagoras," then the conversation is likely to be meaningful. After all, they're someone who can be considerate of your feelings and make compromises accordingly.

t_ch: 你认可「智识」命途吗？
t_en: Do you acknowledge the Path of Erudition?

t_ch: 当你把杂多的存在归纳于一个词语之下，就不得不考虑，它是否会因过于纯粹而远离自己的本质。
t_en: When you categorize diverse existences under a single word, you must consider whether it has become too simplified that it strays from its original essence.

t_ch: 我向来不是个敬神的学者。让我相信所思所想不过是祂们思想的边角料，呵，恕我无法接受。
t_en: I've never been a god-fearing scholar. To believe that my thoughts are merely scraps from THEIR divine intellect? Hah, I simply cannot accept that.

t_ch: 「渎神」这种事…干了一次就会忍不住想干第二次。
t_en: Once you've blasphemed against the gods... You can't help but want to do it again.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 拿着它吧。「贤者之石」——人们曾称它为炼金术至高的冠冕，获得它便能得到此世的一切智慧。但与整个宇宙的知识相比，那不过是冰山一角。
t_en: Take this. This is the "Philosopher's Stone." Once hailed as the supreme crown of alchemy, possessing it meant gaining all wisdom of this world. But compared to the knowledge of the entire universe, it's merely the tip of an iceberg.

t_ch: 没有任何东西能让你获得智慧，除了书籍和教训。
t_en: Nothing can grant you wisdom except readables and lessons.

t_ch: 希望它能让你铭记这一点。
t_en: I hope this will help you remember that.

t_ch: 我也去听卡吕普索说了啥。
t_en: I'll go see what Calypso has to say too.

t_ch: …听听可以，切忌多嘴。
t_en: ...You can listen, but keep your mouth shut.

t_ch: 来，索芙洛尼娅，跟我念，太阳——纺锤——花朵——
t_en: Come on, Sophronia, repeat after me: Sun, Spindle, Flower—

t_ch: 太，阳…纺锤…发、发朵……
t_en: S—Sun... spindle... f—fawer...

t_ch: 不是哦，是花——朵——
t_en: Nope, it's a flo-wer~

t_ch: 你们这是在？
t_en: What are you guys doing?

t_ch: 发朵，发朵！
t_en: Fawer, fawer!

t_ch: 还…还是要念念发朵吗？
t_en: Do... do I still have to say "fawer"?

t_ch: …这个词念花朵，不要被这位{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}带偏啦。
t_en: ...This word is pronounced as "flower." Don't let this {F#Big Sister}{M#Big Brother} lead you astray.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，容我为你介绍，这位芙缇尼是我年少时家中的盲仆，我从她这里学到了很多…包括美，也包括很多人生阅历。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, allow me to introduce you. Futini here was a blind servant in my household during my younger years. I learned a great deal from her... about beauty, and many life experiences.

t_ch: 哪里的事，我可是时常听起大小姐提到{NICKNAME}阁下。
t_en: Not at all, I often hear the young lady mention {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 啊，您就是…阿格莱雅小姐…提到的…英雄吧？原来…是这样的{F#大美人}{M#小帅哥}啊。
t_en: Ah, so you're... the hero... Miss Aglaea... mentioned? I see you're quite the {F#beauty}{M#handsome one} indeed.

t_ch: 而这位是索芙洛尼娅。或许你在奥赫玛城中见过她，那枚髀石护身符就是她送给我的。
t_en: And this is Sophronia. You might have seen her in Okhema. She's the one who gave me this knucklebone talisman.

t_ch: 在你送给翁法罗斯的新世界中，芙缇尼重获了视力，而索芙洛尼娅也拥有了声音。不过，她们还需要一些时间适应各自的礼物。
t_en: In the new world you gave to Amphoreus, Futini regained her sight, and Sophronia found her voice. However, they still need some time to adjust to their respective gifts.

t_ch: 我在教索芙洛尼娅发音，她是个很聪明的小女孩。{NICKNAME}您听得出，刚才那句对您说的话，她已经准备了很久了。
t_en: I've been teaching Sophronia pronunciation. She's a very clever little girl. {NICKNAME}, she has been practicing that line for you for quite a while now, as you can tell.

t_ch: 我…会教芙缇尼…认字。
t_en: I... will teach Futini... how to read.

t_ch: 等你们学完了，就一起去吾师那里喝燕麦粥吧。
t_en: Once you're done studying, let's all go have some oatmeal porridge at Master's place.

t_ch: 啊，提起燕麦粥，我记得小姐您以前因为偷吃偷喝被缇宝大人教训……
t_en: Ah, speaking of oatmeal porridge, I remember when you got scolded by Tribbie for secretly snacking on food and drinks...

t_ch: 阿格莱雅小姐…还有那种事？
t_en: Miss Aglaea... does that kind of thing happen too?

t_ch: 人总有不成熟的时候，那都是什么时候的事了，芙缇尼。
t_en: Everyone has their immature moments. That was ages ago, Futini.

t_ch: …唉，重视彼此的人，通常会把对方不成熟时的样子记得很清楚呢。
t_en: ...Ah, people who care about each other tend to remember very clearly what the other was like in their immature days.

t_ch: 这个词是…「幸运」？
t_en: This word is... "fortunate"?

t_ch: 不…这个词…是「幸福」。
t_en: No... this word... is "felicitous."

t_ch: 能够与珍视之人共享一个光明而悦耳的世界，是多么叫人欣慰的事情啊。
t_en: How comforting it is to share a bright and melodious world with those we cherish.

t_ch: 我很高兴与你分享眼中的世界。
t_en: I'm happy to share the world through my eyes with you.

t_ch: 呵…有浪漫之处，亦有不便之处。
t_en: Heh... It has its romantic aspects, as well as its inconveniences.

t_ch: 不便在于，人心所思所想、掩于神色之下的情绪，诸多本可凭借金丝知晓的细末之事，如今我要重新学着去「阅读」了。
t_en: The inconvenience lies in having to relearn how to "read" all those subtle details... People's thoughts and desires, the emotions hidden beneath their expressions. Things I used to easily discern through the golden threads.

t_ch: 而浪漫之处在于…无论是对比显著的色彩、光泽，抑或布料上最细微的纤绒，都可以靠着人类的双眼看清。
t_en: And what makes it romantic is... whether it's the stark contrast of colors and luster, or the finest fibers on the fabric, they can all be seen clearly with the human eye.

t_ch: 也许，奥赫玛的领袖更加需要能够饱览万物的金丝；而一位普通的织者，却只需要一双明媚的眼眸。
t_en: Perhaps the leader of Okhema needs golden threads that can see all things, while an ordinary weaver only needs a pair of bright eyes.

t_ch: 感觉每次见到阿雅都有新鲜感。
t_en: Meeting Agy always feels like a fresh experience.

t_ch: 对此，我很庆幸。因为只有身为人，才能够不断变化、成长。不同的人会分别记住我的不同面貌——
t_en: I'm grateful for that. Because only as a human can one keep changing and growing. Different people will remember different aspects of me.

t_ch: 芙缇尼和海瑟音记住了我骄纵任性的样貌，黄金裔和公民们目睹我冷静持重的样貌，而索芙洛尼娅和吾师则能看见我柔软之处。
t_en: Futini and Hysilens remember my spoiled and willful side, the Chrysos Heirs and citizens witness my calm and composed side, while Sophronia and my master can see my gentle side.

t_ch: 但无论如何，这些都是阿格莱雅最真实的样子。
t_en: But in any case, this is who Aglaea truly is.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 这枚物件，你一定很熟悉了——那位聋哑女孩送我的髀石护身符，我希望能将它转交给你。
t_en: You must be familiar with this item. It's the talisman made of femoral stone that the non-hearing, non-speaking girl gave me. I'd like to pass it on to you.

t_ch: 虽然它已不再具备墨涅塔的神力，但它依然是最纯粹的「浪漫」象征。希望高洁的灵魂能够永远守护你的旅程。
t_en: Though it no longer possesses Mnestia's divine power, it remains the purest symbol of "Romance." May the spirit of nobility forever guard your journey.

t_ch: 希望你永远这么幸福。
t_en: I hope you stay this happy forever.

t_ch: 谢谢你的祝愿，相信所有人都会永远幸福。
t_en: Thank you for your wishes, I believe everyone will be happy forever.

t_ch: 今天想吃些什么，我亲自下厨。
t_en: What would you like to eat today? I'll be the chef.

t_ch: 石榴汁烧嘟噜鸟排！那味道还真是忘不了啊。
t_en: Pomegranate juice grilled Dulu bird cutlets! I just can't forget that taste!

t_ch: 孩子他爸，别愣着呀，你想吃什么。
t_en: Darling, don't just stand there. What would you like to eat?

t_ch: …我和你母亲吃一样的菜，迈德漠斯。
t_en: ...I'll have the same as your mother, Mydeimos.

t_ch: …怎么着你们俩，菜单上可没有「……」啊，禁止沉默！
t_en: ...What's with you two? "..." isn't on the menu, don't just say nothing!

t_ch: 你好，我这边可以点菜吗？
t_en: Hi there, can I place my order?

t_ch: 哦！要演亲情大戏了吗？
t_en: Oh! Are we about to witness a family drama?

t_ch: …是你啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...Oh, it's you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你是迈德漠斯的朋友吧？哈哈，这些天有太多人来拜访了，真想不到他人缘这么好。
t_en: You're Mydeimos's friend, right? Haha, so many people have been visiting these days. Who knew he has so many friends?

t_ch: 不如，你也留下来一起吃顿饭吧？放心，我们没闹家庭矛盾。
t_en: Why don't you stay and join us for dinner? Don't worry, we're not having any family drama.

t_ch: 我们只是刚知道，有一世的迈德漠斯…受了许多苦。
t_en: We just found out that a past Mydeimos... suffered a great deal.

t_ch: 悬锋人的字典里，没有「受苦」二字。
t_en: There is no word for "suffering" in the Kremnoan language.

t_ch: 悬锋的字典还在变薄。
t_en: Kremnos's dictionary keeps getting thinner.

t_ch: 菜谱里也没有。
t_en: It's not in the recipe book, either.

t_ch: 历史的走向，或许只在人的一念之差。
t_en: The course of history can change in the blink of an eye.

t_ch: 最后一世，在多洛斯平原与凯撒一战后，我与歌耳戈都认可了逐火的使命，这才使悬锋的荣耀不再囿于纷争的血泪。
t_en: In my last life, after the battle with the Imperator on the Plains of Dolos, both Gorgo and I acknowledge the mission of Flame-Chase, finally freeing The Glory of Kremnos from the tears and blood of Strife.

t_ch: 悬锋的存续…未必大于世界存亡。前世的我犯了太多错误，无论是用毒计杀死你，还是将迈德漠斯抛入冥海……
t_en: The survival of Kremnos... may not be worth more than the fate of the world. In my past life, I made too many mistakes, whether it was poisoning you, or casting Mydeimos into the Sea of Souls...

t_ch: 好了，别追忆了。先前我从欧洛尼斯的祭司口中套出了些关于你的趣事，你要是在新世界还忆苦思甜的，小心我和族人们分享哦。
t_en: Alright, enough with the reminiscing. I managed to get some juicy stories about you from the Oronyx priest earlier. If you keep getting all nostalgic in the new world, I might just share them with the people.

t_ch: 你说的…是什么记忆？
t_en: What... memory are you referring to?

t_ch: 咳，你曾说…「我与歌耳戈是在血尘弥漫的角斗中相识相爱……」
t_en: Ahem, you once said... "Gorgo and I fell in love amidst the blood-soaked dust of the arena..."

t_ch: 别说了，我认错，饶了我吧。
t_en: Stay thy tongue! I confess my fault, now prithee, grant me clemency.

t_ch: 好温馨的互动感。
t_en: Such a heartwarming interaction.

t_ch: 有点肉麻了哦？
t_en: Getting a bit mushy, aren't we?

t_ch: 他们就是这样，让你见笑了。
t_en: That's just how they are, sorry you had to see that.

t_ch: 留下与我们共进晚餐吧，也讲讲那些星空的故事。想吃点什么？
t_en: Stay for dinner with us and share those stories of the starry skies. What would you like to eat?

t_ch: …歌耳戈，你知道的，无论哪一世，我都想做一个合格的王…和一位合格的父亲。
t_en: ...Gorgo, you know, in every life, I've always wanted to be a worthy king... and a worthy father.

t_ch: 这么小声干嘛，和迈德漠斯当面说去。至少在最后，你如愿成为了更好的人啊。
t_en: Why are you whispering? Go tell Mydeimos to his face. At least in the end, you became a better person, just like you wanted.

t_ch: 等急了么？晚饭马上就好。
t_en: Getting impatient? Dinner's almost ready.

t_ch: 没有纷争的无聊世界…
t_en: A boring world without strife...

t_ch: 在我看来，纷争正是为终结纷争而存在。纵是此世必要之痛，亦有愈合的一天。
t_en: In my view, strife exists to end strife itself. Though it may be a necessary pain in this life, there will come a day of healing.

t_ch: 何况，做好每顿饭，与夺下战功同样光荣。
t_en: Besides, preparing each meal is just as glorious as winning battles.

t_ch: 如今这个世界不缺本应灭绝的生物，食材丰富了许多。对任何热衷烹饪的人来说，都称得上是足够有趣。
t_en: These days, the world is full of creatures that should have gone extinct, which has greatly enriched our ingredients. It's quite fascinating for anyone passionate about cooking.

t_ch: 黄金大饭店要开业了吗？
t_en: Is The Grand Restaurant about to open?

t_ch: 可以挥着菜刀上战场了。
t_en: Time to charge into battle with a kitchen knife.

t_ch: 哈哈，你的话语，还是一如既往地天马行空啊。
t_en: Haha, your words are still as wild and fanciful as ever.

t_ch: 你还恨欧利庞吗？
t_en: Do you still hate Eurypon?

t_ch: 前世，我的父亲在谋害母亲前曾说：「我与歌耳戈是在血尘弥漫的角斗中相识相爱…那么，便也让我在角斗中，亲手与她作别吧。」
t_en: In my past life, before father murdered mother, he said: "Gorgo and I met and fell in love in the blood and dust of battle... So, allow me to bid her farewell personally in battle once more."

t_ch: 这一世，他也说过相同的话…但，那是在与我母亲并肩作战，一同在抗争来古士的战场上牺牲之时说的。
t_en: In this life, he said the same words... but that was when he fought alongside my mother, sacrificing himself on the battlefield in defiance against the Lygus.

t_ch: 至于我的答案…想必你也清楚了。
t_en: As for my answer... I'm sure you already know.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 带上这盏酒杯吧。每位曾和我并肩作战的伙伴，都有与我碰杯的资格。
t_en: Take this wine glass. Everyone who has fought by my side has earned the right to share a toast with me.

t_ch: 当你在那遥远的星间，踱行于自己的道路上时，若偶感沮丧，便向翁法罗斯的方向举杯吧。随时随刻——
t_en: When you're wandering among those distant stars, treading your own path, should you ever feel down, raise a glass toward Amphoreus. Anytime—

t_ch: 歌耳戈之子，迈德漠斯，向你致意。
t_en: Mydeimos, son of Gorgo, sends his regards.

t_ch: 我要吃——饭！
t_en: I need to eat! Food!

t_ch: 好，希望你的胃口能和你的嗓门一样大。
t_en: Alright, let's hope your appetite is as big as you say it is.

t_ch: 奶奶快看我手里这些宝贝——哀地里亚的银戒，厄涅俄努斯的陶瓷，还有上好的银簪子…我从奥赫玛最好的裁缝铺里挑的，你戴上一定好看。
t_en: Grandma, look at these treasures in my hands. Silver rings from Aidonia, ceramics from Aenionus, and these fine silver hairpins... I picked them from the best tailor's shop in Okhema, they'll look beautiful on you.

t_ch: 拿上吧，你以后再也不用起早贪黑，去集市上受那帮挑三拣四的客商们的气啦！
t_en: Take it! No more getting up at the crack of dawn to deal with those picky merchants at the market giving you grief!

t_ch: 做生意讨生活的事可不能说是受气啊，话又说回来，这么多好东西，你不会是……
t_en: Hey, doing business to make a living isn't about taking abuse, but come to think of it, with all these nice things, you're not...

t_ch: 呃…是好心人听说我的丰功伟业之后，主动送来的，可热情了。我不要，他们还硬往我兜里塞呢。
t_en: Uh... some kind folks heard about my great achievements and eagerly brought these over. I tried to refuse, but they kept insisting on stuffing them in my pockets.

t_ch: 赛法利娅，看着我的眼睛。
t_en: Cifera, look into my eyes.

t_ch: 奶奶您可别不信啊，我还带了证人来——巴特鲁斯，快说点什么！
t_en: Granny, you've gotta believe me! I even brought a witness. Bartholos, say something!

t_ch: 呃…打、打扰了，奶奶！
t_en: Uh... e—excuse me, grandma!

t_ch: 桀、桀桀，大、大姐头说的句句都是实话！
t_en: Heh, heh heh, B—Boss lady's speaking nothing but the truth!

t_ch: 哎呀你刚才还信誓旦旦的，怎么这时候笨嘴拙舌起来了？灰子，你也替我说两句！
t_en: Hey, you promised so confidently just now, why are you suddenly tongue-tied? Gray Mystery, help me out here!

t_ch: 赛飞儿说的句句都是大实话。
t_en: Cipher's speaking nothing but the truth.

t_ch: 奶奶，灰子可是大英雄，{F#她}{M#他}说一句顶别人一百句呢。
t_en: Granny, Gray Mystery is a real hero. {F#Her}{M#His} words carry more weight than a hundred others combined.

t_ch: 赛飞儿是受人景仰的大英雄！
t_en: Cipher is a legendary hero that everyone looks up to!

t_ch: 哼，没错。要不是我，黄金裔们早就被黑潮打垮啦。
t_en: Hmph, that's right. If it weren't for me, the Chrysos Heirs would've been crushed by the black tide long ago.

t_ch: 赛法利娅，你这个小机灵鬼，一定吃了不少苦头，才练出这样一番伶牙俐齿吧？
t_en: Cifera, you clever little thing! Must've been through quite a lot to develop such a sharp tongue, huh?

t_ch: 无论是谎言还是真相，只要是赛法利娅说的，奶奶都会相信。
t_en: Whether it's lies or truth, granny will believe anything Cifera says.

t_ch: 呃…嘿嘿…奶奶，我可是忍不住会撒点小谎的，你可别全信啊。
t_en: Uh... hehe... Granny, I can't help but tell little white lies sometimes, so don't take everything I say at face value.

t_ch: 不过嘛…我向您保证，我的英雄事迹可都是有目共睹的，先从哪段讲起呢？
t_en: But well... I can assure you, my heroic deeds are well-documented. Now, which story should I start with?

t_ch: 说说多洛斯侠盗大战扎格列斯？
t_en: How about that showdown between the Dolos Rogues and Zagreus?

t_ch: 就从黎明机器的惊天骗局开始？
t_en: Shall we start with the Dawn Device's mind-blowing scam?

t_ch: 唔…唔…赛法利娅，这差事听起来可不简单，你没有受伤吧？
t_en: Hmm... hmm... Cifera, that job sounds pretty rough. You didn't get hurt, did you?

t_ch: 别操心啦，奶奶。那帮家伙，连碰到我衣角的能耐都没有~
t_en: Don't worry, granny. Those guys don't even have what it takes to touch the hem of my clothes~

t_ch: 呵呵…不愧是我的孙女。奶奶替你和朋友们热些驼奶，你就着酥皮点心慢慢说。
t_en: Hehe... that's my granddaughter for you. Granny will heat up some camel milk, and you can tell me all about it while enjoying these flaky pastries.

t_ch: 恭敬不如从命了。
t_en: Your wish is my command.

t_ch: 你好，我吃一点！
t_en: Hi, I'll have a bite!

t_ch: 咦，也、也有我们的份吗？谢谢奶奶！
t_en: Huh, there's... there's some for us too? Thank you, grandma!

t_ch: 嘿嘿……
t_en: Heh heh...

t_ch: 我还有好多~好多精彩的冒险故事呢，等我慢慢讲给你听啊……
t_en: I have sooo~ many more exciting adventure stories to tell you, just let me share them with you slowly...

t_ch: 要我说，那些张扬跋扈的权贵确实应该教训教训。你不知道啊，小葡萄，奶奶我年轻的时候……
t_en: If you ask me, those arrogant bigwigs definitely need to be taught a lesson. You don't know this, little grape, but back in my day...

t_ch: 咦？！奶奶你——
t_en: Huh!? Grandma, you—

t_ch: 呵呵呵……
t_en: Heh heh heh...

t_ch: 像那句老话说的，多洛斯不养闲人！
t_en: As the old saying goes, Dolos has no room for slackers!

t_ch: 灰子，要说随机应变，关键时刻还是得靠你！
t_en: Gray Mystery, when it comes to thinking on your feet, you're the one we can count on in a pinch!

t_ch: 回家的感觉如何？
t_en: How does it feel to be home?

t_ch: 有吃有穿，晚上还有暖融融的被窝。这还是人过的日子吗，这简直是元老们过的日子！
t_en: Food to eat, clothes to wear, and a cozy warm bed at night. Is this even how normal people live? This is living like the Elders!

t_ch: 让我都有些想起在裁缝女的裁缝铺打零工那段时光了……
t_en: It reminds me of that time when I worked part-time at the tailor's shop...

t_ch: 不过扎格列斯有云，硬币都有两个面。每次早晨想睡懒觉，奶奶都会来掀被子，晚上要出门工作的时候，又得听她的千叮咛万嘱咐。
t_en: But as Zagreus says, every coin has two sides. Every morning when I want to sleep in, grandma comes to pull off my blanket, and when I need to go out for work at night, I have to listen to her endless nagging and reminders.

t_ch: 她对我们这一行，缺乏最基本的尊重嘛！
t_en: She has zero respect for what we do!

t_ch: 最近收成还好吗？
t_en: How's the harvest been lately?

t_ch: 别提了，那一世我用谎话替奥赫玛延续千年白昼的事，已经被大伙传开了。
t_en: Don't even mention it. Everyone's already spreading the story about how I used lies to extend Okhema's eternal day in that lifetime.

t_ch: 现在他们家里少了点什么物件，都会转念一想：「赛飞儿大人这么做，一定有她的原因吧！」
t_en: Now whenever something goes missing from their homes, they just think to themselves: "Lady Cipher must have her reasons for doing this!"

t_ch: 这跟我有任何一点关系吗！害得我必须以「不偷」来对抗「偏见」了。
t_en: What does this have to do with me at all!? Now I have to fight against preconceived notions by not stealing.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 咦，灰子，你、你怎么说出这么肉麻的话！
t_en: Huh? Gray Mystery, h—how could you say something so mushy!

t_ch: 需要提醒才能记住的东西，恐怕本来就没有记住的必要吧？
t_en: If you need a reminder to remember something, maybe it wasn't worth remembering in the first place, huh?

t_ch: 如果你实在想留个纪念，就拿着这枚猫咪硬币吧，反正我也用不上它了。
t_en: If you really want a souvenir, take this cat coin. It's not like I have any use for it anyway.

t_ch: 别忘了我们啊，灰子！
t_en: Don't forget about us, Gray Mystery!

t_ch: 回聊回聊。
t_en: Let's chat later!

t_ch: 好，我再吃些酥皮点心，你也别客气啊。
t_en: Alright, I'll have some more pastries, and don't be shy. Help yourself!

t_ch: 孩子们，先吃点小饼干休息一下吧。圣女大人的住所前已经排起长队了。
t_en: Kids, let's take a break and have some cookies. There's already a long line forming outside the Holy Maiden's residence.

t_ch: 不嘛。*我们*现在就要见到妈妈！
t_en: No way! We want to see mom right now!

t_ch: 我看看怎么个事。
t_en: Let me see what's going on.

t_ch: 不想吃的小甜饼可以给我。
t_en: If you don't want your cookies, I'll take them.

t_ch: 哇！是小小灰！
t_en: Wow! It's Little Gray!

t_ch: 啊，不好意思让您见笑了，孩子们吵着要见圣女大人，但排队的实在是太多了。
t_en: Ah, sorry for the embarrassing scene. The kids were begging to see the Holy Maiden, but the queue was just too long.

t_ch: 因为有很多的*我们*，所以*我们*三个已经等了好久好久。
t_en: Because there are so many of us, the three of us have been waiting for a very long time.

t_ch: 你是说…其他的｢缇宝｣？
t_en: You mean... other "Tribbies"?

t_ch: 真的有那么多人？
t_en: Are there really that many people?

t_ch: 没错，排在我们前面的有缇宋、缇宇、缇官、缇赛、缇宫、缇宙、缇容……
t_en: That's right, ahead of us there's Trissha, Triyew, Tricome, Trissy, Trigone, Tricorne, Trilawney...

t_ch: 停——停！
t_en: W—Wait!

t_ch: 欸。小灰别急，缇宝老师还没念完呢。
t_en: Hey. Patience, Little Gray. Miss Tribbie hasn't finished reading yet.

t_ch: 搁这报菜名呢？
t_en: What is this, a restaurant menu?

t_ch: 小小灰，我知道你很饿，但*我们*不能吃啦！
t_en: Little Gray, I know you're hungry, but we aren't edible!

t_ch: 这下可以人手一只了。
t_en: Now everyone can have one.

t_ch: 小灰又在开玩笑了，*我们*可不是玩偶呀。
t_en: Little Gray is joking again, we aren't dolls, you know.

t_ch: 哎呀，总而言之，*我们*已经等很久啦。再不快点的话，今天就见不到妈妈了。
t_en: Oh dear, long story short, we've been waiting forever. If we don't hurry up, we won't get to see Mom today.

t_ch: 那就明天见……
t_en: See her tomorrow...

t_ch: 不！今天，就要见！
t_en: No! We'll see her TODAY!

t_ch: 啊！难道小灰也是来排队的嘛？不许插队哦！
t_en: Ah! Little Gray, are you here to queue up too? No cutting in line!

t_ch: 我的年纪比你小，让我先！
t_en: I'm younger than you, let me go first!

t_ch: 您就别开玩笑啦，这里已经忙不过来了。
t_en: Come now, stop joking around. We're already swamped here.

t_ch: 好好，不和你们抢。
t_en: Fine, fine, I won't fight you for it.

t_ch: 所以，*我们*有点忙，很可能， 没时间陪小小灰玩啦。先道歉！
t_en: So, we're a bit busy, and most likely won't have time to play with Little Gray. Sorry!

t_ch: 那就，明天再见啦。
t_en: Well then, see you tomorrow!

t_ch: 明天见——
t_en: See you tomorrow—

t_ch: 看着孩子们，总是让我回想起以前服侍圣女大人的时候。
t_en: Watching these kids always reminds me of when I served the Holy Maiden.

t_ch: 圣女大人可喜欢我做的小甜饼了…只不过后来担心会得蛀牙，我就偷偷把甜饼藏了起来。
t_en: The Holy Maiden really loved my cookies... but later I got worried about cavities, so I secretly hid them away.

t_ch: 可嘴馋的圣女大人，每次都能找到我藏的甜饼…看着怎么也藏不住，就只好放任她吃了。不过我们也约定好，晚上睡觉前要好好刷牙。
t_en: But our sweet-toothed Holy Maiden always manages to find my hidden cookies... Since there's no way to keep them hidden from her, I've just given up and let her have them. Though we did make a promise — she has to brush her teeth properly before going to bed at night.

t_ch: 啊…不好意思，自顾自地回忆起来了。下次过来，我也给您准备点吧。
t_en: Ah... sorry about that, I got lost in my memories for a moment. Next time you come by, I'll prepare something for you.

t_ch: 呀~是小灰！
t_en: Ah~! It's Little Gray!

t_ch: 缇宝老师…
t_en: Miss Tribbie...

t_ch: 嗯？怎么啦小灰，有什么问题要请教缇宝老师吗？
t_en: Hmm? What's up, Little Gray? Got any questions for Miss Tribbie?

t_ch: 明天见真是最伟大的预言吗？
t_en: Is "see you tomorrow" really the greatest prophecy?

t_ch: 听你这么说，这一定是前世的*我们*教给你的话吧？
t_en: From what you're saying, I believe it was our past life selves who taught you that. Am I right?

t_ch: *我们*知道，有些人觉得这不是预言，而是谎言。因为没有人能确定，大家真的能在明天相见。
t_en: We know some people think this isn't a prophecy, but a lie. Because no one can be certain that we'll all meet again tomorrow.

t_ch: 但是，*我们*仍然相信预言的力量。
t_en: However, we still believe in the power of prophecy.

t_ch: 当说出「明天见」的时候，不仅仅意味着*我们*希望能够在明天相见，也会为了能在明天相见而努力。
t_en: When we say "see you tomorrow," it means not only that we hope to meet tomorrow, but also that we'll make an effort to make sure that meeting happens.

t_ch: 所以，「明天见」…其实也是一种自我实现的预言啦！
t_en: In other words, "see you tomorrow"... is also a self-fulfilling prophecy!

t_ch: 人们为何期盼着明天呢？
t_en: Why do people look forward to tomorrow?

t_ch: *我们*明白你在想什么，小灰。
t_en: We know what you're thinking, Little Gray.

t_ch: 的确，没有人知道明天会发生什么，也没有人能保证明天会变得更好。
t_en: Indeed, no one knows what tomorrow will bring, and no one can guarantee that tomorrow will be better.

t_ch: 但是，只有到了明天我们才能知道，过去埋下的｢种子｣，究竟会开出怎样的花，结出怎样的果实。
t_en: But only until tomorrow will we know what kind of flowers will bloom and what kind of fruits will bear from the "seeds" we planted in the past.

t_ch: 今天，有没有变得比昨天更好，明天，又会不会变得比今天更好？
t_en: Have we become better today than yesterday, and will tomorrow be even better than today?

t_ch: 这些答案和意义，只能在一次次走向明天的过程中找到啦！
t_en: These answers and meanings can only be found as we keep moving forward toward tomorrow!

t_ch: （尝试捏捏缇宝的脸）
t_en: (Try to pinch Tribbie's cheeks)

t_ch: 呜哇！小灰！突…突然间做什么啦？
t_en: Woah! Little Gray! Wh—What are you doing all of a sudden!?

t_ch: 发、发生什么了？
t_en: Wh—What happened?

t_ch: 脸颊…有点痒痒的……
t_en: My cheeks... itch a little...

t_ch: 对不起，就是想试试…
t_en: Sorry, I just wanted to try that out...

t_ch: 小灰！要对老师保持尊敬啦，冷不丁的，吓了*我们*一跳！
t_en: Little Gray! You have to show some respect to your teacher! You gave us quite a scare, doing that without a warning!

t_ch: 原来你们的感觉真是互通的。
t_en: So all of you are really interconnected.

t_ch: 当然啦！每次缇安偷偷吃酸酸的东西，我都能感受到。
t_en: Of course! I can feel it every time Trianne sneaks some sour snacks!

t_ch: *我们*之间，没有秘密！
t_en: Between us, there are no secrets!

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 当然有啦！锵锵！这是*我们*亲手缝制的娃娃，怎么样，很可爱吧？
t_en: Of course! Ta-da! This is a doll that we made by hand. Adorable, isn't it?

t_ch: 如果，小小灰想我们了，就捏一捏，娃娃吧！
t_en: If you ever miss us, just give the doll a little squeeze, Little Gray!

t_ch: 担心晚上会做噩梦的话，就抱着它入眠吧。
t_en: If you're worried about having nightmares, just cuddle it in your arms.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，*我们*希望能像天上的星星那样，散布在每个你失眠的夜晚。而现在，小灰也有一颗属于自己的星星啦！
t_en: Hehe, we hope to be like the stars in the sky, scattered across every sleepless night of yours. And now, you have a star of your very own, Little Gray!

t_ch: 下次再和你们玩！
t_en: Let's play again next time!

t_ch: 好呀。小灰想玩的时候，要记得来找*我们*！
t_en: Sure! When Little Gray wants to play, remember to come find us!

t_ch: 真是片祥和的花园啊，没有刀剑，没有火焰…新世界的律法，竟是这般仁慈？
t_en: What a peaceful garden this is! No swords, no flames... Are the laws of this new world really so merciful?

t_ch: 也好，我准许它的存在。比起颂歌与塑像，臣民的笑语才是对君王至高的赞美——看，剑旗爵他们正相谈甚欢呢。
t_en: Very well, I shall permit its existence. Rather than hymns and statues, the laughter and chatter of subjects are the highest praise for a ruler... Look, Dux Gladiorum and the others are having quite a delightful conversation.

t_ch: 你不去讲两句吗？
t_en: Aren't you going to chat with them?

t_ch: 或许他们也在等你加入。
t_en: Maybe they're waiting for you to join them.

t_ch: 呵…不必了。爱臣私语的话所，君王应保持沉默。我借走了他们的人生，也该让他们好好放个长假了。
t_en: Heh... no need. Rulers should stay silent in places where their subjects hold private conversations. I've borrowed their lives, so it's only fair to let them take a nice, long vacation.

t_ch: 知道吗？我并非生来便流淌着金血。它就像君王的权柄，是我靠这双手抢来的。
t_en: You know what? I wasn't born with golden blood. Like a king's authority, I seized it with these hands.

t_ch: 我用尽手段、留下暴君的骂名，是因为若要铲除不公，只有这一条路能走通。
t_en: I've used every means at my disposal and earned the name of tyrant. That was because to root out injustice, this was the only path forward.

t_ch: 但若是可以，我希望世人不必费力登上山巅，也能过想要的生活。听说天外的群星中，确有几颗如此温柔。
t_en: But if possible, I hope people won't have to struggle to climb mountains just to live the life they want. I hear there are a few stars out there beyond the sky that are gentle enough for that.

t_ch: 现在，这个夙愿算是实现了大半吧。新生的翁法罗斯，定将迎来更有温度的律法。
t_en: Now, this long-cherished wish has largely come true. The reborn Amphoreus will surely embrace laws with more warmth and humanity.

t_ch: 说得我有些感动了…
t_en: Aww, you're making me feel all emotional...

t_ch: 能和我逐一辈子火吗？
t_en: Can you spend the rest of your life chasing the flame with me?

t_ch: 哈哈。我听出了你话语中的僭越，但无妨，毕竟我是人们口中的「僭主」。
t_en: Haha. I hear the insolence in your words, but it's fine. After all, I am what people call a "tyrant."

t_ch: 果然，独白还是要有听众才行——不是为我谋划的幕僚，不是任我摆布的棋子，也并非什么救世主，只是一位愿意倾听的朋友。
t_en: Indeed, a monologue needs an audience... not a strategist plotting for me, not a pawn at my disposal, nor some Deliverer. Simply a friend who's willing to listen.

t_ch: 谢谢你，主爵。
t_en: Thank you, Dux Primarius.

t_ch: 无需在意礼数，畅所欲言吧。
t_en: Don't worry about formalities, just speak your mind.

t_ch: 关于我的爵位…
t_en: About my peerage title...

t_ch: 我考虑再三，觉得还是「主爵」这个头衔最适合你。
t_en: After careful consideration, I think the title "Dux Primarius" suits you best.

t_ch: 此美称我构思了很久。你既是救世主，亦是这段故事的主角。一石二鸟，岂不合适？
t_en: I've pondered this title for quite a while. You're both the Deliverer and the protagonist of this story. Killing two birds with one stone. Isn't that fitting?

t_ch: 考虑到你的贡献，我还要赐你「凯撒的恩典」——从今往后，翁法罗斯任何餐厅、旅馆和浴场，都不得向你收取费用。
t_en: In recognition of your contributions, I hereby grant you the "Imperator's Grace." From this day forward, all restaurants, hotels, and baths in Amphoreus shall provide their services to you free of charge.

t_ch: 我准许你将这个头衔带去星海，此种特权在其他星球享有同等效力。
t_en: I hereby grant you permission to carry this title across the stars. This privilege holds equal authority on all other planets.

t_ch: 若有人问起，就说这是由翁法罗斯的凯撒颁赐的——呵，要开具证明的话，就让他们亲自来谒见我吧。
t_en: If anyone asks, tell them it was bestowed by the Imperator of Amphoreus. Heh, and if they want proof, let them come and seek an audience with me themselves.

t_ch: 之后有什么打算吗？
t_en: Any plans after this?

t_ch: 往后么…我打算给自己放个长假，顺带重拾些因征战而抛下的爱好。
t_en: Moving forward... I plan to take a long vacation and pick up some hobbies I had to put aside during the conquests.

t_ch: 我曾梦想做个棋士，四处云游，以棋会友…可惜翁法罗斯不能一日无君，这个想法也就一直被我搁置了。
t_en: I once dreamed of being a chess master, traveling the world, and making friends through the game... But alas, Amphoreus can't go a day without its ruler, so that idea has been shelved away.

t_ch: 若是可以，我想与天外的棋士交手，那一定极为有趣。
t_en: If possible, I'd love to play a game with chess masters from beyond the sky... That would be incredibly fascinating.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 当然有——我的「后」棋。
t_en: Of course there is. My "Queen" piece.

t_ch: 「后」是最强的棋子，来去如风，势不可挡。希望你今后的旅程，也能如它一般自由。
t_en: The Queen is the most powerful chess piece, moving like the wind, unstoppable in its path. May your future journey be just as free as it is.

t_ch: 群星会传颂凯撒的征途。
t_en: The stars shall sing of the Imperator's conquest.

t_ch: 当然。毕竟我要征服的，可是这星辰大海啊。
t_en: Of course. After all, I'm here to conquer the seas of stars.

t_ch: 爸、妈，大家…我回来了。
t_en: Dad, Mom, everyone... I'm home.

t_ch: 白厄…你…你长高了。
t_en: Phainon... you... you've grown taller.

t_ch: 哈哈…这么久不见，是不是都快认不出我了？
t_en: Haha... It's been so long. You almost didn't recognize me, did you?

t_ch: 怎么会呢，妈第一眼就认出你了。还是那个愣头愣脑，只知道闷头往前的傻孩子！
t_en: Of course not, mom recognized you at first glance. Still the same scatterbrained kid who just barrels ahead without thinking!

t_ch: 希洛尼摩斯，瞧瞧他长得多结实。刚离家时还满脸稚气，现在啊，他的个头都要把你给比下去咯。
t_en: Hieronymus, look! He's grown so strong. He was all baby-faced when he first left home, but now, he's even taller than you!

t_ch: 这是好事啊，要不然，怎么配得上「救世主」的名号呢？
t_en: That's a good thing! Otherwise, how could he live up to the title of "Deliverer"?

t_ch: 喂——爸，妈！
t_en: Hey— Mom, Dad!

t_ch: 乡亲们的消息真灵通啊。
t_en: Word sure travels fast around here.

t_ch: 我们的救世主脸红了吧？
t_en: Is our Deliverer blushing?

t_ch: 呵呵，我也没想到，当年嚷嚷着要去悬锋城学剑的你，有一天竟然能击败尼卡多利！
t_en: Heh, who would've thought? Back then, you were all about "wanting to learn swordsmanship at Castrum Kremnos!" And now look at you, you've actually beaten Nikador!

t_ch: 再让你这臭小子下地照料庄稼，还真有些大材小用了。
t_en: Having a talented kid like you work the fields would be a real waste of potential.

t_ch: 哈哈…爸，这也不是我一个人的功劳。
t_en: Haha... Dad, I can't take all the credit for this.

t_ch: 能走到今天，多亏了一路上结伴而行的朋友，我的老师、我的对手，还有…最好的伙伴。
t_en: I couldn't have made it this far without the friends who walked this path with me: my teachers, my rivals, and... my best companion.

t_ch: 没错，正是在下。
t_en: That's right! That's me!

t_ch: 我们是好搭档！
t_en: We're great partners!

t_ch: 这是个很长的故事…我会慢慢讲给你们听。
t_en: It's a long story... I'll tell you all about it slowly.

t_ch: 我家这小子，一定让你费心照顾了！快，我做了些蜂蜜烙饼，千万别客气。
t_en: This kid of mine must have been quite a handful! Here, I made some honey suncakes. Please, help yourself!

t_ch: 白厄，在外边见过了大世面，可别瞧不上妈的手艺哦。
t_en: Phainon, I know you've seen the world out there, but don't you dare look down on your mom's cooking!

t_ch: 怎么会，我想念哀丽秘榭的菜好久好久了！
t_en: No way, I've been craving Aedes Elysiae's food for ages!

t_ch: 唉…我家披索，要是能有白厄一半省心就好了。
t_en: *sigh*... If only my Piso could be half as well-behaved as Phainon.

t_ch: 我就知道，老爸又要拿我开涮了。
t_en: I knew it, Dad's gonna make fun of me again.

t_ch: 呵呵，披索，你之前不是成天念叨着白厄哥的吗？现在怎么反倒不好意思起来了？
t_en: Heh, Piso, weren't you always going on and on about Phainon before? Why so shy about it now?

t_ch: 皮西厄斯老师冤枉人，我哪有！
t_en: Mrs. Pythias, that's not true. I didn't say any of that!

t_ch: 我证明！你一天念叨好几回呢。还说等白厄哥回来…一定要让他把这些年的冒险故事全讲给你听！
t_en: I can vouch for that! You bring him up several times a day. You even said when Phainon comes back... you'll make him tell you all about his adventures from these past years!

t_ch: 呃…等他吃饱了蜂蜜烙饼，才有力气讲故事嘛！
t_en: Uh... let him finish his honey suncake first, and then he'll have the energy to tell his stories!

t_ch: 搭档，也来尝尝我爸妈的手艺吧。
t_en: Partner, come try my parents' cooking.

t_ch: 一切都结束了。
t_en: It's all over.

t_ch: 哈哈，多亏了你。如果不是你肩负起这份重任，我恐怕还在绝境里徒劳徘徊吧？
t_en: Haha, all thanks to you. If you hadn't shouldered this burden, I'd probably still be wandering aimlessly in desperation, wouldn't I?

t_ch: 得到…然后失去，历经挣扎…最终徒劳而返。
t_en: To gain... and then to lose. To struggle... only to end up with nothing.

t_ch: 我已经习惯那些面孔在我面前短暂地停留，然后匆匆离去。
t_en: I've grown used to those faces briefly lingering before me, and then hastily departing.

t_ch: 说来有些可笑，恍惚间，我还是会怀疑这是不是一场梦…在麦田里醒来后，是不是又得面对一场漫无尽头的旅程？
t_en: It's almost laughable... Sometimes in a daze, I still wonder if this is all just a dream... After waking up in that wheat field, will I have to face another endless journey?

t_ch: 放心，大家都在你身边。
t_en: Don't worry, we're all here by your side.

t_ch: 是啊，有你，还有我们的伙伴们。
t_en: Yeah, I've got you and all our buddies.

t_ch: 别留下心理创伤了。
t_en: Don't let it traumatize you.

t_ch: 旅程才刚结束，几位老师就忙不迭地送我去昏光庭院诊断了。缇宝老师担心火种会对身体产生影响，那刻夏老师…可能只是纯粹感兴趣。
t_en: As soon as the journey ended, they rushed me to the Twilight Courtyard for a diagnosis. Lady Tribbie was worried about the Coreflame's effects on my body, while Professor Anaxa... was probably just purely curious.

t_ch: 医嘱是：多聊天、多锻炼，多吃饭、多睡觉。
t_en: The doctor advised me to chat more, exercise more, eat more, and sleep more.

t_ch: 现在，我没有继续愁眉苦脸的理由了。是时候翻开新的一页了！
t_en: Now, I have no reason to keep moping around. It's time to get a fresh start!

t_ch: 可以好好居家修养了。
t_en: Time to get some proper rest at home.

t_ch: 哈哈，回家前，总想着能好好歇息一阵子，但很快就会发现有不少事等着去做。
t_en: Haha, before heading home I thought I could get some good rest, but I quickly found out there's quite a lot to be done.

t_ch: 替老爸砍柴火，为伽尔巴叔叔制弓，和皮西厄斯老师讲讲树庭的见闻…还有闹着让我讲故事的披索和莉维娅，要满足他们的好奇心，可得费些功夫。
t_en: Chopping firewood for Dad, making bows for Uncle Galba, sharing tales from the Grove with Mrs. Pythias... And then there's Piso and Livia, always pestering me for stories. It takes quite some effort to satisfy their curiosity.

t_ch: 对了，还得去探望迷路迷境的妖精们…许久不见，它们恐怕又稀里糊涂地把日子过得一团糟了吧？
t_en: Oh right, I should also visit the fairies in the Membrance Maze... It's been a while. I bet they've made a complete mess of things again in their usual scatterbrained way?

t_ch: 哈哈，未来很长一段时间，怕是都有得忙了。
t_en: Haha, looks like we'll have our hands full for quite a while to come.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 原先，我想把「侵晨」托付给你，但它见证了太多无谓的杀戮，恐怕不是件合适的礼物。
t_en: Originally, I wanted to entrust Dawnmaker to you, but it has witnessed too much pointless bloodshed. I fear it wouldn't make for an appropriate gift.

t_ch: 正巧哈托努斯在铸造它时，曾打造过一批仿件，这把就给你留作纪念吧，伙伴。
t_en: As luck would have it, Chartonus made a batch of replicas while forging it. I'll let you keep this one as a memento, partner.

t_ch: 好好歇会儿吧。
t_en: Take a good rest.

t_ch: 嗯。期待与你再次相会…伙伴。
t_en: Hmm. Looking forward to meeting you again... partner.

t_ch: 嗷呜嗷呜~荒笛大人，奇拉拉总算追随上你的脚步了。
t_en: Awoo awoo~ Lord Terravox, Chirara has finally caught up with you!

t_ch: 老友，这小奇美拉是…？
t_en: Hey buddy, what's up with this little chimera?

t_ch: 我不小心掉到过地穴深处，是荒笛大人的声音引领我找到了出口，要不然，奇拉拉早就重新变成「大地」的一部分了，嗷呜嗷呜~
t_en: I accidentally fell deep into the caverns, but Lord Terravox's voice guided me to the exit. Otherwise, Chirara would've already become one with the Earth again, awoo awoo~!

t_ch: 呵呵，那你应该记得我说过「我想让大地上的生物各行其是」，你为何又回到了我身边？
t_en: Heh, you probably remember I said "I want to let all creatures on Earth live as they please," so why did you come back to me?

t_ch: 因为…奇拉拉想听荒笛大人和「开山者」大人从前的征战故事！听说那时候的大地兽凶猛善战，不光是像我这样的小奇美拉，连山之民都要小心它们的脾气呢。
t_en: Because... Chirara wants to hear Lord Terravox and Lord "Mountainbreaker" tell stories about their past battles! I heard that back then, the dromases were fierce warriors. It's not just little chimeras like me, even the Mountain Dwellers had to be wary of their temper!

t_ch: *凶猛的叫声*呜嗡~
t_en: *Fierce growl* Grumph~

t_ch: 哟！别激动，说起来，那的确是让人热血沸腾的往事啊！
t_en: Yo! Take it easy there! But yeah, those were some seriously blood-pumping memories!

t_ch: 你好，开山者，我是开拓者。
t_en: Hi, Mountainbreaker, I'm the Trailblazer.

t_ch: 啊，久闻你的名号，还是第一次见面。
t_en: Ah, I've heard so much about you, but this is our first time meeting.

t_ch: 瞧你的眼神，莫非也对「大地」的族裔过去的历史感兴趣？
t_en: That look in your eyes... Are you also interested in the history of Earth's descendants?

t_ch: 我也想听，给我听听。
t_en: I want to listen to it too. Tell me about it.

t_ch: 哈哈，难得有人还对「大地」的族裔过去的历史感兴趣。
t_en: Haha, it's rare to find someone who is still interested in the history of Earth's descendants.

t_ch: 那时候，山之民豢养的小奇美拉也有着长长的角，是灵活的冲锋选手，每逢开战时，大地兽在前面踏起滚滚尘土，遮蔽敌人的视线……
t_en: Back then, the little chimeras raised by the Mountain Dwellers also had long horns and were agile chargers. During combat, the dromases would kick up clouds of dust at the front, obscuring the enemy's vision...

t_ch: 而小奇美拉就从后面蹿出，发起突刺冲锋，伴随着骁勇的山之民先锋队，没有任何敌人能够抵挡我们的战术协同！
t_en: Then, the little chimeras lunge from behind with a piercing charge, alongside the valiant Mountain Dweller vanguard! No enemy could withstand our tactical coordination!

s_ch: 奇拉拉&兴奋的小大地兽
t_ch: 嗷呜~嗷呜嗷呜~
t_en: Awoo~ Awoo awoo~

t_ch: 小家伙怎么也嗷呜上了。
t_en: Why's the little one howling too?

t_ch: *兴奋的叫声*嗷呜嗷呜~
t_en: *excited howling* Awoo awoo~

t_ch: 噢，差点忘了你，小家伙。
t_en: Oh, almost forgot about you, little one.

t_ch: 你就好好锻炼筋肉，努力长大，等长大后才能成为山之民的好帮手。
t_en: Just keep working on those muscles and grow up strong. Once you're all grown up, you can become a reliable helper to the Mountain Dwellers.

t_ch: 嗷呜~嗷呜嗷呜~
t_en: Awoo~ Awoo awoo~

t_ch: 老友，{F#她}{M#他}这是何意？
t_en: Hey, old friend, what's {F#she}{M#he} getting at?

t_ch: 你果然和我在「永夜之帷」的记忆中看到的一样…活泼。
t_en: You're just as lively as I saw in my memories of the Veil of Evernight...

t_ch: 哈哈，但那位护卫「开拓」的朋友，却是个稳重内敛的人。也许就像从前的你和我，不，他们比起你和我…嗯…要更心意相通些。
t_en: Haha, but that friend who defends the Trailblaze is quite the reserved and composed one. Perhaps like how you and I used to be... No, they're actually... hmm... more in sync with each other than we ever were.

t_ch: 呜嗡~能解开从前的误会，已经是「大地」赐给我们的宽容了。
t_en: Grumph~ Being able to resolve past misunderstandings is already a gift of tolerance from Earth to us.

t_ch: 老友，听你说起这些事情，好像回到了从前结伴征战的岁月里。不过，现在的翁法罗斯，再也不需要战争啦！
t_en: Old friend, hearing you talk about these things brings me back to the days when we fought side by side. But now, Amphoreus has no need for war anymore!

t_ch: 这可是好事，你和我不也总是聊起战争结束之后的事吗？现在它已经来了，我反倒是没有做好准备……
t_en: This is a good thing. Haven't you and I always talked about what would happen after the war? Now that it's here, I find myself unprepared...

t_ch: 现在，我只想做一个尽职尽责的大地兽饲养员。把你照顾好，还要把这些小家伙洗刷喂养大。
t_en: Right now, I just want to be a dedicated dromas caretaker to take good care of you and raise these little ones by washing and feeding them.

t_ch: 这就是我们征战时渴求的，最平常简单的幸福啊……
t_en: This is the simple happiness we've been longing for during our battles...

t_ch: 呜嗡~荒笛大人，说不定从前也只有我这么大，吃了很多红土，才长成一座山那么大的。
t_en: Grumph~ Lord Terravox, maybe you were once as small as me too, and only grew as big as a mountain after eating lots of redsoil!

t_ch: 嗷呜嗷呜，奇拉拉也多吃红土，能变得腿更长，更好追上荒笛大人的脚步吗？
t_en: Awoo-woo! If Chirara eats more redsoil, will my legs grow longer so I can keep up better with Master Terravox?

t_ch: 哈哈！那我这个饲养员也得提高业务水平了。总之，先咨询一下「奇兽学派」的科学喂养法吧，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Haha! Looks like I'll have to work on my zookeeper skills. First things first, let's check out the scientific method of feeding based on "cryptozoology."

t_ch: 呜嗡~和老友叙叙旧，陪新结识的「忘年交」们玩耍，我从前渴求过的幸福，就是如此简单。
t_en: Grumph~ Catching up with old friends, hanging out with my new "close buddies"... The happiness I've always longed for was really this simple all along.

t_ch: 「开山者」变得开朗了好多。
t_en: Mountainbreaker has become much more cheerful.

t_ch: 我悄悄和你说一桩事，你可不要说我揭老友的短处。
t_en: I'll let you in on a secret, but don't snitch on me for exposing my old pal's weakness.

t_ch: 吉奥刻勒斯他，常常为从前的那桩背叛痛心，对那位奥赫玛的君王——刻律德菈，态度总是有些别扭。
t_en: Geocles is often heartbroken over that betrayal from the past, and his attitude toward the ruler of Okhema, Cerydra, has always been somewhat awkward.

t_ch: 好比…他从不和「凯撒」打招呼，但每逢「凯撒」与人对弈，他却忍不住过去旁观，甚至想要「锐评」一番。
t_en: For instance... he never greets the Imperator, but whenever the Imperator plays a game of chess with someone, he can't help but watch from the sidelines. He's even tempted to leave harsh critiques.

t_ch: 我也喜欢高手对弈！
t_en: I love watching pros go against each another!

t_ch: 是啊，我曾经也有幸与「凯撒」对弈过一番，那确实是位深谋远虑的伟人。
t_en: Yeah, I once had the honor of playing a game with the Imperator. She is indeed a woman with impressive foresight and intricate strategies.

t_ch: 在那之后，我也成了热衷观棋的一员。
t_en: After that, I also became one of those chess-watching enthusiasts.

t_ch: 他希望「凯撒」输吗？
t_en: Does he want the Imperator to lose?

t_ch: 不，他希望「凯撒」赢，他不接受战胜过自己的「凯撒」输给任何人。
t_en: No, he wants the Imperator to win. He won't accept she would lose to anyone else after besting him.

t_ch: 这不是口嫌体正直吗？
t_en: So his words and actions don't match?

t_ch: 「凯撒」下棋没输过，老友的纠结也没停过。
t_en: The Imperator has never lost a game of chess, and her old friend has never stopped fretting either.

t_ch: 假如有机会，我想亲自开解他们当中的芥蒂，说不定，他们还能成为棋友？
t_en: If the chance arises, I'd like to help clear up the misunderstanding between them. Who knows? They might even end up becoming chess buddies.

t_ch: 有结识新的人类朋友吗？
t_en: Have you made any new human friends?

t_ch: 是说黄金裔们？回想起上次轮回，我早早沉入大地深处，与多数英雄们几乎不曾有过照面。
t_en: You mean the Chrysos Heirs? Come to think of it, during the last cycle, I descended deep into the earth much earlier, so I barely crossed paths with most of the heroes.

t_ch: 但逐火的后辈当中，听说有位学者对大地兽格外偏爱，甚至为它们发表了《论大地兽的美德》等多篇论文。
t_en: Among the younger Flame-Chasers, there was a scholar who was especially fond of dromases, and even published several papers including The Virtues of Dromases.

t_ch: 呵呵，我也很愿意与他结识一番，前提是…他对大地兽的爱并非「叶公好龙」呐！
t_en: Hehe, I'd love to get to know him too, but only if... his fondness isn't restricted to the mere concept of them, all talk and no substance!

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 当然，我没忘记要把这样东西郑重地交给你，和如今那位「大地」的新神——
t_en: Of course. I haven't forgotten to hand this over to you solemnly, and to that new God of "Earth"—

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的持明卵？
t_en: An Amphorean Vidyadhara egg?

t_ch: 你也有蜕生之法？
t_en: Are you also capable of hatching rebirth?

t_ch: 呵呵，这只是一枚普通的大地兽蛋。
t_en: Hehe. It's just a regular dromas egg.

t_ch: 从前我和老友吉奥刻勒斯一同仰望过树庭的「星空」，每一颗星星都意味着一片更为深远的土地，我对天外群星的向往也从那时开始……
t_en: Long ago, I gazed at the "starry sky" of the Grove with my old friend Geocles. Each star represented a distant land, and that's when my yearning for the stars beyond the sky began...

t_ch: 倘若有一天它能孵化，从中诞生的新生命，或许是翁法罗斯第一个真正步入银河的生命吧？
t_en: If it hatches one day, perhaps the new life born from within will be the first Amphorean life to truly step into the cosmos?

t_ch: 辛苦你守望了昨日。
t_en: Thanks for keeping watch over yesterday.

t_ch: 呜嗡~也感谢你们守护了我的心愿。昨日的守望，都是为了一起走向明天。
t_en: Grumph~ Thank you all for protecting my wishes too. We watch over yesterday to walk toward tomorrow together.

t_ch: 得闲时来我的肩膀上坐一坐，一起离那片星空更近一些吧。
t_en: When you have time, come and sit on my shoulder. Let's get a bit closer to those stars together.

t_ch: 哈，拉比努斯，你的肚子还撑得住吗？看来这次是我要赢咯。
t_en: Ha, Labienus, can your stomach still handle it? Looks like I'm gonna win this one.

t_ch: 呃…我只是喝了个水饱。都怪这里的蜜酿太稀了！
t_en: Ugh... I just drank too much water. It's all because the honey brew here is too watery!

t_ch: 既然如此，要不要尝尝最浓郁的蜜酿？
t_en: In that case, would you like to try the richest honey brew?

t_ch: 黑潮退去后，我回到故乡，把法吉娜埋进海床的十二宝瓶都挖了出来。
t_en: After the black tide receded, I returned to my homeland and dug up all twelve bottles that Phagousa had buried in the seabed.

t_ch: 今日，{RUBY_B#十二宝瓶之一}冰泉原浆{RUBY_E#}不限量畅饮，共同喝个尽兴吧。
t_en: Today, have as much {RUBY_B#one of the twelve bottles}Essence of Icy Spring{RUBY_E#} as you wish! Let's drink to our heart's content!

t_ch: 不、不限量畅饮？这么珍贵的东西……
t_en: A—As much as I wish? But it's something so precious...

t_ch: 当年法吉娜把它们埋进海床，本就是为待黑潮退去后，与子嗣们共饮。如今拿来庆祝不是正好？
t_en: Back then, Phagousa buried them in the seabed because she wanted to share them with her descendants after the black tide had receded. Aren't they perfect for a celebration now?

t_ch: 剑旗爵盛情难却，我们就恭敬不如从命了。小维吉妮娅，今天恐怕有你发挥的机会了。
t_en: Since Dux Gladiorum insists so passionately, we'll humbly accept the offer. Little Verginia, looks like you'll get your chance to shine today.

t_ch: 那么，这一杯…敬我们的昨日与明日。
t_en: Well then, here's to our yesterdays and tomorrows.

t_ch: 逐火之旅，干杯！
t_en: Cheers to the Flame-Chase Journey!

t_ch: 敬新世界！
t_en: To the new world!

t_ch: 敬翁法罗斯！
t_en: To Amphoreus!

t_ch: …唔…痛、痛快！法吉娜的窖藏，果然名不虚传！
t_en: Mmm... W—Wow! That really hit the spot! Phagousa's collection really lives up to its reputation!

t_ch: 入口虽然有些酸涩，但只要稍微含一会，就能感受到淡淡的甜味…还有股海风的气息。
t_en: Though it tastes a little astringent at first, if you let it sit in your mouth for a moment, you can taste a subtle sweetness... and a hint of sea breeze.

t_ch: 嗯，味道确实不错。可我们身为人臣，哪有独自享用的道理？去把凯撒大人请来。
t_en: Hmm, the taste is exquisite indeed. But as loyal subjects, how could we enjoy this by ourselves? Go and invite the Great Imperator.

t_ch: 晚些时候我会去找她对酌，几位就请尽享此刻吧。不尽兴不归哦。
t_en: I'll join her for drinks later. For now, please enjoy yourselves to the fullest. And remember: We're not leaving until our cups run dry!

t_ch: 那好，今天我要成为站到最后的赢家，下一个挑战者是谁？
t_en: Alright then, today I'll be the last one standing! Who's my next challenger?

t_ch: 上吧，维吉妮娅。
t_en: Your time to shine, Verginia.

t_ch: 那我…我来！
t_en: Then I'll... I'll do it!

t_ch: 小灰鱼，也与我共饮一杯吧？瞧——
t_en: Little Gray Fry, care to join me for a drink? Look...

t_ch: 神血蜜露，你应该还记得它的味道。不记得也没关系，我可以帮你回忆起来。
t_en: The Dew of Divine Blood, you should still remember its taste. Though, it's fine if you don't, I can help you recall it.

t_ch: 干杯，小灰鱼儿。
t_en: Cheers, Little Gray Fry.

t_ch: 干杯，海瑟音。
t_en: Cheers, Hysilens.

t_ch: 这…不…可…能……
t_en: This... is... impossible...

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}阁下，你快帮帮塞涅卡！刚才她和我比赛喝、喝蜜酿，结果没几杯就要撑坏了！
t_en: Oh, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, please help Seneca! She was having a drinking contest with me. W—We were drinking honey brew, but she's about to hit the floor after just a few cups!

t_ch: 没几杯…
t_en: Just a few cups...

t_ch: 到底喝了几杯？
t_en: How many cups did you have, exactly?

t_ch: 我在旁边数着呢，整整五十二杯。
t_en: I was keeping score. A whopping total of <unbreak>52</unbreak> cups.

t_ch: 冬霖爵，好肚量，可惜对上了小维吉妮娅这样的怪物。你输得不冤！
t_en: Dux Brumalis, you drink well, but it's unfortunate you ran into a monster like little Verginia. There's no shame in this loss!

t_ch: 胡说…我…还没有输…我还能再战……
t_en: Nonsense... I'm... not done yet... I can still drink on...

t_ch: 看来这场庆功宴，要持续很长一段时间了，哈哈！
t_en: Looks like this victory feast is gonna last for quite a while, haha!

t_ch: 小灰鱼，还要再来一杯吗？
t_en: Little Gray Fry, want another drink?

t_ch: 这个地方还不赖吧？
t_en: Not too shabby here, huh?

t_ch: 再创世之后的世界…很温柔，就像一场永不落幕的宴会，所有人都能举起陶杯，共饮蜜酿。
t_en: The world after Era Nova... is very gentle, like an endless banquet where everyone can raise their porcelain cups and enjoy honey brew together.

t_ch: 我一直在追求的，不就是这样一处归宿吗？可当我真正游入港湾之后，心里剩下的，只有平静。
t_en: Isn't this the kind of home I've been hoping for all along? Yet now that I've finally arrived at this harbor, all I feel is peace.

t_ch: 大概是因为我在游向这里的途中，已经找到属于自己的火焰了吧。
t_en: I guess it's because on my way here, I've already found my own flame.

t_ch: 对了，有个美中不足的地方：这里的水域有些狭小，偶尔会挥舞不开鱼鳍呢。
t_en: Oh right, there's one small downside: The water bodies here are rather narrow, and sometimes I can't stretch my fins as freely as I'd like.

t_ch: 你肚子里为什么全是水？
t_en: Why is your stomach full of water?

t_ch: …呵。我从你的眼神里看出，这个问题你很早就想问了，小灰鱼儿。
t_en: ...Heh. I can see it in your eyes. You've been wanting to ask that for a while now, Little Gray Fry.

t_ch: 因为我是「海洋」的女儿。这副身躯，本来就是由灵水捏塑出来的。
t_en: Because I am the Ocean's daughter. This body of mine was originally molded from spirit water.

t_ch: 那你跑步的时候会打嗝吗？
t_en: Do you get hiccups when you run?

t_ch: 那你不喝水液面会下降吗？
t_en: So does your water level drop when you don't drink?

t_ch: …小灰鱼实在好奇的话，不如与我共同觅食一遭？或许，你能亲眼看到答案呢。
t_en: ...Little Gray Fry, if you're that curious, why not join me for a hunt? Perhaps you'll find the answer with your own eyes.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 值得铭记的东西…小灰鱼是打算把它带去天外之海吗？也好……
t_en: Something worth remembering... Little Gray Fry, do you plan to take it to the sea beyond the sky? That works too...

t_ch: 这枚骨螺来自斯缇科西亚的海床，是我许多年前采撷的，历经风霜仍保持光洁。
t_en: I collected this shell fossil from the seabed of Styxia many years ago, and it still retains its pristine shine despite weathering through snow and storms.

t_ch: 请务必好好珍藏——不如，就摆在你的床头吧？
t_en: Make sure to treasure it well. How about displaying it by your bedside?

t_ch: 愿下一场宴席永不散场。
t_en: May the next feast be eternal.

t_ch: 没关系。宴席结束之后，我们也将奔赴新的宴席，不是么？
t_en: Don't worry. After this feast ends, we'll be off to another one, won't we?

t_ch: 遐蝶姐姐，你有没有试过挽起头发盘成发髻，或者梳起刘海扎成发辫？
t_en: Hey Castorice, have you ever tried putting your hair up in a bun, or braiding your bangs?

t_ch: 啊…盘成…发髻？我还没有试过。
t_en: Ah... putting my hair... up in a bun? I haven't tried that before.

t_ch: 嗯，遐蝶姐姐的头发很漂亮…想象了一下，应该会很适合的。
t_en: Hmm, your hair is so pretty... I imagine it'll fit you perfectly.

t_ch: 之前在哀地里亚，晚祷以后，孩子们为彼此梳洗头发时，总看到你坐在一边看着。
t_en: Back in Aidonia, I always saw you sitting aside as you watched the children groom each other's hair after evening prayers.

t_ch: 所以我总是在想，你是不是也想要试试呢？
t_en: So I'd always wondered, would you like to give it a try too?

t_ch: 唔，我熟识的姐姐，也是个喜欢打扮，每天用鲜花点缀头发的爱美孩子呢。
t_en: Oh, my dear sister also loves to doll up. She's someone who takes pride in her appearance, and decorates her hair with fresh flowers every day.

t_ch: 小玻吕茜亚，我…也没有每天吧……
t_en: Little Polyxia, I... I don't do it every day...

t_ch: 原来你是这样的遐蝶！
t_en: So this is who you really are, Castorice!

t_ch: 如果可以我也想换个发型。
t_en: I wish I could change my hairstyle too.

t_ch: 阁下的话，感觉试试{F#扎个高马尾}{M#把额头露出来}，应该挺不错的。
t_en: For you, I think {F#a high ponytail}{M#showing your forehead} would look quite nice.

t_ch: 说起来，自担任哀地里亚的督战圣女以来，我大部分时候都是自己一人编发的…毕竟想要麻烦别人替我梳理头发，并不是那么容易。
t_en: Come to think of it, ever since I became Aidonia's Maiden of War, I'd been styling my own hair most of the time... After all, it's not easy to trouble others to help me with that.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人曾经帮我梳理过几次，但我也不希望自己那微渺的愿望，将她置于险境。
t_en: Lady Aglaea has helped groom me up a few times before, but I wouldn't want my trivial wishes to put her in harm's way.

t_ch: 但是现在，一切都不一样了。
t_en: But now, everything is different.

t_ch: 是啊，这其中也多亏了阁下所付出的努力，我很感谢你。
t_en: Indeed, this was all thanks to your efforts as well. I'm truly grateful to you.

t_ch: 其实那个时候，我也可以帮你…
t_en: Actually, I could have also helped you back then...

t_ch: 阁下，不要总是拿「那时自己已经死了」来开玩笑啦。
t_en: Please stop joking about how you were "already dead back then."

t_ch: 诅咒已经解除啦。现在，你可以无忧无虑、自由自在地去实现自己的心愿。
t_en: The curse has been dispelled. Now, you can freely fulfill your wish without a worry in the world.

t_ch: 即便这心愿再微小，但…这就是我希望看到你的样子！
t_en: No matter how small that wish may be... this is exactly how I wanted to see you!

t_ch: 我们可以替你编头发，每天都不重样哦。在哀地里亚圣殿，我可学到了不少呢。
t_en: We can style your hair differently every day. I learned quite a few ways at the Aidonia Temple.

t_ch: 呵呵…背祷文时磕磕绊绊，但记这些旁支末节时，倒是过目不忘呢。
t_en: Heh... You stumble through your prayers, but when it comes to these trivial details, you've got a photographic memory.

t_ch: 啊，阿蒙内特大人？！
t_en: Ah, Lady Amunet!?

t_ch: 呵呵，就罚你去给你的遐蝶姐姐编头发吧。
t_en: Hehe, as punishment, you'll have to go braid your sister Castorice's hair.

t_ch: 太好了！
t_en: Yay!

t_ch: 咦…那、那就多谢你们了。
t_en: Huh... Th—Thank you all.

t_ch: 就从麻花辫开始吧！我来编左边！
t_en: Let's start with braids! I'll do the left side!

t_ch: 那么，我就编右边吧。
t_en: Well then, I'll take the right side.

t_ch: …那我怎么办？
t_en: ...What am I supposed to do then?

t_ch: 我来喊加油吧。
t_en: I'll cheer you guys on.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下…抱歉，让你见笑了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm sorry you had to witness this.
